reviews.review_title: Can finally play Steam flat games 🖥️
reviews.review_description:
Yes, I know there’s Virtual Desktop, Air Link, wired Link, etc. 😅 But those methods are just too demanding for my aging 2020 gaming laptop ...

This HDMI Link app has been a game changer 🙌 It lets me connect seamlessly to my PC and use it as a HUGE curved monitor! Great 1080p image quality at around 50 FPS, and the experience feels surprisingly smooth, without ANY of the weird blurry artifacts that come from streaming a PC wirelessly ✨

A couple quick tips:
• Make sure to DISABLE auto-detect in Quest Game Optimizer.
• If you’re getting a black screen, try plugging your Hagibis capture card’s USB-C cable in the other way around 🔄

The app works really well overall 👍 I genuinely hope Meta keeps updating and maintaining it, because this is something the Quest ecosystem seriously needed 🚀

Most of the 1* reviews are by people that don't understand. This app works fine, if it doesn't, it is more likely due to the hdmi capture card having issues, rather than this app. I am using hagibis hdmi capture card and no issues

reviews.review_title: This app works VERY GOOD! if Done Right!
reviews.review_description: I had a bunch of issues getting this to work, then i read the FAQ! Some usb cables use usb 2  and usb 3, if you are getting 1 fps like i was, flip the cable! It must be flipped on the headset side ! This was the game changer! I hope this helps!!

reviews.review_title: Very useful, beware its beta though!
reviews.review_description:
(Updated my review and knocked down original rating since updated to HorizonOS v85 update)

I use a Hagibis USB 3.0 Capture Card with PD (female HDMI port on it) on a Quest 2. PD is important if want to keep powering the headset! Video looks sharp and can take any input from any HDMI output device (not 3D players which is expected) which is cool.

However, video playback is quite jittery. This may be because of the heavy HorizonOS now used or because of frame mismatch to the headset as you cant change its refresh rate of the home environment. The app reports SuperSpeed PCM16 2Ch. 48192 YUYV 1920x1080@65fps and framerate oscillates around there.

Would like to see the following added:

- Would like to be able to change the capture settings manually instead of having to unplug and replug in the hagibis everytime.
- Ability to alter framerate of headset so can choose relevant Hz for content. Eg: 72Hz for 24p content, 60Hz or 120Hz for 30p/60p content, or 120Hz/90Hz for 30p etc.

This is a good start (as long as have a compatible usb device!) and would like to use it more.
Would like 4k support since my capture card does 4k. Could be a quick and easy update ?
It didn’t work I connected both the hdmi adapter and the hdmi to my phone and the oculus but it said plug in the usb-c cable to my capture card
What's the point of this app when its so horrible ?

It does technically work, at 15fps 720p, and less than 1fps at 1080p. But that's not because of the technology. the Quest can obviously stream 120fps at 4K for Oculus Link over USB3, and Skybox can stream 4K 60fps wireless, and Virtual Desktop can do 120fps. The only way this ended up so bad had to be Meta intentionally making it unusable to drive more revenue through their in-store content. Greed ruins everything. :-(

Give us 4K 
Give us all the features that you can give us 
￼

Update 30/01/2026: It started working again. Plugged in my Bluray Player and Steam Deck no issues with Framerate. Not sure what I / they did to get it to work. It just goes to show the app is very temperamental. And since it has been in listed under the experimental tab for a long time, they are probably not invested in keeping it stable. 


First Review: This was a good app until they decided to break it with an update. Because it feels deliberate. Setting permissions for USB no longer works.
I've installed this application and it works great

I just recently used this App with a  Guermok 4K USB3.0 Video Capture Card with PD 100W USBC charging port, 2K@30FPS or 1080P@60FPS HDMI to USB-C Capture Card for Streaming, direct to my PS5( no PC needed). The HDMI cord I got was long enough to connect the MQ3 to my PS5 and was comfortable.
 I found that if I waited to connect the charging cord until I launched the HDMI link app and started my PS5, the black screen issue was not there. I'm very happy with this App and I use it A LOT!
It works, but the lag is just enough you can't play first person shooters. I don't know if that's a limitation of my capture card or the quest or the software.

It does the job of connecting your hdmi compatible devices to the quest 3 but there's a connection option that's missing and it's 3D. This app doesn't recognise 3D devices so please could we have an update to make it possible.

Works great on my headset,crisp smooth and no lag , not like when im streaming.
I did have an issue at first with it not connecting,black screen and freezing while plugged to a laptop, it was very hit or miss,turned out it was a faulty quest 3s headset because not long after that the headset died ,tracking started going then it just went into a boot loop  even with force wipe and resets, had to be replaced, now with this new headset not had any problems yet.
Not working with Quest 3

I don’t know why people keep saying it doesn’t work. I’ve been using it since it came out with all my systems. I can’t even play my p5 without it. It works with all my cords. Even the cheapest one. You’re not gonna get 4k and 1080p 60 is fine for me. Point is it works.

reviews.review_description: It’s a pretty nice way too use quest 3. I’m using it with switch 2 and it works with 60 FPS without noticeable latency.

You need to make sure you have the right card and cable

I first got this app about a month ago and had brought the U Green capture card and had the same problems everyone has been facing and I came across a helpful review saying the app works perfectly with a hagibis capture card thanks u to the reviewer and I went out and brought the hagibis capture card that let's u plug in a charger to it also witch it will say supports 100w PD and I plugged it to my blue ray player and in to the meta quest 3 and it came on straight away I was so happy when I got to see it work it's 100% just the capture card u use it's definitely not the app the app works fine thanks meta for an amazing app and it's hagibis capture card with 100w PD u need guys and all will be well hope this helps guys take care god bless

reviews.review_title: Another great value add for Quest 3
reviews.review_description:
This is another example of Meta providing continual updates and support to their products over the years. It works great! 

The only thing I would want to have in addition is Frame Packing and SBS display support.  

This would enable me to connect my old PS3 to the Meta Quest and play some old 3D games on it . ^_^
I can forgo a little quality for better compatibility!!🫩 I am bewildered at how frustrating such desirable tech can be.

reviews.review_description: At first everything was working fine. Then j closed the app and tried to reopen it later and all I get is a black screen. Uninstalled then reinstalled, restarted my device and quest, triple checked all the cables. Still not working
I’ve purchased various cables. The screen just goes dark. Both headset and phone have the most recent updates. Was the main reason I bought the headset to watch movies from iPhone on long plane rides

reviews.review_title: Not working with UGREEN capture card
reviews.review_description: Tested multiple times with the UGREEN capture card model 25854 and it doesn't work.

Of all the simple or long winded complicated tech things I’ve done I’ve very rarely plugged something in, launched a programme or app and got what I expected rather than what I was given. Now watching films in 1920 x 1080 saved to HD from the extended screen hdmi out on my i3 windows 10 £349 Acer, 8 year old laptop
PS before I retired during COVID I project managed expensive home cinema builds in large houses, come across more problems with HDMI than I care to remember. Rs

I had access to this app a few updates back but now my unchanged setup wont work. This is so frustrating and a big dropped ball by meta. Get this working or at least open up options to tell me why It won't work. I've used my meta half as much now because of this one thing I was so excited for.

I use this regularly with a Hagibis USB 3.0 Video Capture Card with PD charging. Even with the latest firmware (V79) this app still works for me. I suspect that the UGreen card everyone is using is the problem, not the software.

Very happy with the app.
Top

Just tried this for the first time in a while, it used to work brilliantly with my switch and ugreen capture card, but as other reviews have noted the feature is bricked with an error saying it can’t support the selected settings. Same hardware worked fine about a month ago

With ugreen USB video video capture card it now fails to work with v74 of Quest 3 firmware. You need to set three permissions: Camera Yes, Record Audio Yes, USB No; shows as USB permission denied. This needs to be fixed. 

This is a problem with v74 not the video capture card as it worked before and is seen by Quest 3

i waste my money on multiple types of capture cards 
Meta pls solve this problem ASAP

This is great for connecting anything with an HDMI output, such as a Blu-ray player for watching movies. I do think a lot more could be done to improve the app, but for now I'm quite happy with it.

When using this app for movie watching I had a GREAT idea! With so many movies supporting Dolby 5.1,  Dolby 7.1, and Dolby Atmos Surround Sound, it would be AWESOME if the Meta Quest VR headsets could connect wirelessly to a home cinema surround sound speaker system. So the VR would provide the visuals, and the speakers provide the surround sound 😃 Meta could even manufacture a specially made speaker system for this which could be purchased separately. An idea for Meta Quest 4 or 5 perhaps 😉

The Meta HDMI App is good though, and an essential download for all Meta Quest 3 owners!

reviews.review_title: Works great but could use a bit more
reviews.review_description: After first testing this with the switch model, I have I checked to see if it would support surround sound through translation of LPCM into the quest, 3-D spatial sound by creating three virtual objects that would act as each channel. I don’t know how hard that is to put in, but would be a neat feature. Plus you’d be able to use your quest headset as the surround sound system so you don’t have to go ahead and buy some stupidly expensive one. It’s an interesting idea with the spatial surround 3-D features built into the quest by default so kind of a missed opportunity. But if you can’t do it, then I understand.

reviews.review_title: Not working in v78 with GREEN cap-card
reviews.review_description:
Not working in v78 with GREEN capture card.
Does this software have high compatibility requirements for video capture cards or HDMI cables?

This app is a good idea but it has several problems :
* Works when it wants to. A headset reboot does help. 
* The settings can't be accessed or modified once the app is launched.
* The theater view is too big for a 1080p resolution. It would be great to have a resizable view (kinda like what has been done for the environment lighting).
* We need a third party tool (the capture card). It would be great if meta could sell it, or (even better) support HDMI over WiFi (with an HDMI dongle that generate its own wifi network for example.

An even greater feature would be support for DisplayPort over USB or Wi-Fi, with hardware emulation that allows direct interaction with the device. I’m aware of solutions like Steam Link, Meta Quest Link, and Virtual Desktop, but they fall short in terms of lag, stability, compression quality and GPU overhead.

They are rumors of such a device for the next valve headset.
This is a great idea for anything but the oculus. Wasted money on a HDMI cable for false hope
It's amazing app, but I would love to be able to play my PS5 in 3D using this app.

It's great, I really wish we could curve the screen when not using cinema mode. Please add the option for SBS 3D, similar to virtual desktop.
Please allow us to have more control of the screen size, and how far we have the screen.

reviews.review_title: Works well except on main desktop
reviews.review_description: I think my issue with the desktop not displaying well might have something to do with Windows trying to display at one resolution for my main display, and a different one for the display inside of VR. All of that said, whenever I launch an application in full screen, it works pretty much how I’d expect it to. I’ve also tested it with the Nintendo Switch, which for the most part is more stable than Windows. It’s pretty cool to be able to play my steam games with only a little bit of latency. Used with the Meta Quest 2 and a Razer Ripsaw HD capture card.

It worked nice I got 1080p with 60 frames then it froze when I started it back up it said I could only do 1080p with 50 frames and I was like that's not a big deal but if I play it more than 5 minutes it just freezes and I've tried it a lot and I've just got to the point where I've given up on using it until it gets majorly updated

Got the app got the card plugged ps4 in and boom. It worked straight away no issues. Next step all excited. Put 3d bluray in and the ps4 said its not a 3d display. 2 hours later after going on my rig and downloading the obs for the capture card thinking direct to pc may work and the ps4 says the same. So all we need is for the quest hmd to tell devices that its capable of receiving 3d content and we're onto a winner. After 3 years of hoping for 3d movie store or an app like this capable of plugging in direct to the hmd by hdmi to watch 3d blurays and its still a big let down. The app works great though with a £20 game recorder off amazon. The absolute lack of 3d films ie bluray films available to watch in a user friendly way in the uk in vr is actually like a cosmic joke where anyone who actually takes genuine joy in this marvellous format has to watch reduced quality nock off versions streamed on big screen cos even bigscreen can't get the licence to show 3d movies in the UK or is prohibited by the sheer cost of it. The devs of this app are going in the right direction  but guys! Slip a bit of code in that verifies the hmd can play 3d and test it against consoles and players with 3d bluray playback and we 3d videophiles will pay you £10 for the app add on. Make it quick though this fiasco has almost broke my spirit

reviews.review_title: What I Hoped For - With a Caveat
reviews.review_description:
Using Hagibis capture card (as recommended by several YouTube videos), and a usb-c extension to have less dangling going on at the headset. 
I wanted to use my PS5 while the TV is being used and this didn't disappoint. 

Playing Silent Hill 2 remake, I got consistent 60fps, 1080p. However, don't expect to multi-task without frames dropping. Having a web page open in the window next to it seemed to have an effect that made the game drop to 30-40 fps. Haven't tried to replicate the issue, but it was noticed, for sure.

I got the Hagibis 4k@60fps to 1080p@60fps, just make sure to plug the USB-C the right way, if you are getting 30fps or less, flip the USB-C it only works at 60fps on one side.
But if I unplug the capture card and I plug it back, it doesn't work, I have to restart my headset for it to work again, I don't know, also If I want to go back to the resolution list to change it, it can't go back when I select the back arrow, I figured out that swiping the screen left would sometimes take me back go settings, but it feels not solid, please imrprove the UX a little more, thanks.
This is the right way to go. It would be great if the Quest 4 had HDMI support and you no longer needed a capture card to connect devices to it.
Hagibis capturecard
Would be amazing if this app could support side by side video etc. The switch has a few VR games that output this over HDMI and I could play them on a decent headset.

reviews.review_title: Bad Capture Window Size on UGreen CM716
reviews.review_description: I want this to work so badly. But , the HDMI capture shows up like a picture-in-picture, mini version.  Doesn't show full screen, the rest of the window is black a kind of grey / green.  Looks like that part of the buffer is not initialized correctly.  Probably an issue with not initializing to the correct capture resolution, mixing up max capture capability (4K) vs actual screen resolution setting.

reviews.review_title: Has potential, but Low FPS issue
reviews.review_description:
I honestly hate to give a low rating, because this is an incredibly useful app, but the performance seems to be quite variable - with the exact identical capture card, some users are getting great performance, while others (myself included) are perpetually stuck at < 8 fps. With my capture card, my laptop recording Switch runs smoothly, so I don't think it's an issue with the capture card itself.

I tried with multiple devices connected to my Quest 3 (PC, Switch, Steam Deck), all with horrible framerates.

There aren't any settings to adjust in this app itself, so nothing to tweak and experiment with. If Meta can figure out why there's such a drastic discrepancy between users running the same capture card and resolve the issue, I'll gladly update this review to a higher rating.

developer_response.body: Thanks for your feedback. We are rolling out a change to select capture video format, resolution and fps supported by the card and app. Also, make sure you are using USB 3.0 speed (SuperSpeed) cables. Some USB-C cables operate at USB 3.0 or USB 2.0 depending on the orientation of the connector; flipping the USB-C cable upside down may also resolve the low fps issue.

reviews.review_title: The app works fine, needs settings menu
reviews.review_description: Such a lost opportunity. I've said the Quest headsets, AND the Go headset, needed this feature ever since Facebook went down the standalone path. The success of these devices would have absolutely exploded if this was one of the core features from the very beginning. I'm happy Facebook/Meta is trying to innovate in the virtual world space, but they should have taken a more hardware focused approach to their headsets as displays, first and foremost. The OS should have a "dumb" mode, simply allowing basic OS functionality and interactivity, with the ability to connect any video source to the headset. At least, now this ability exists, but the lack of development on this feature all these years is extremely apparent. I gave 4 stars because it works. It isn't perfect. And it just highlights the flaws of not thinking about this when developing the hardware to begin with. I avoided the Quest headsets for a very long time due to the lack of connectivity options, but once I had a steam deck and a quest headset the very first thing I thought of was "can I play my steam deck inside my headset, on an airplane or while at sea with no internet?" All these years, every Quest owner with a Switch, a Steam Deck, a laptop, a phone with video out, iPads and tablets and a massive number of devices, definitely wanted this feature. I suggest releasing an official dongle, providing more settings options within the app, and some kind of a walkthrough and troubleshooting to help new users, as well as perhaps a relaunch of the app, or just integration with the main OS. This should be a main selling point for the Quest headsets, not some hidden features only a few people will even hear about.

Unfortunately it doesn't work for me. I'm stuck in a loop repeatedly getting the request to grant permission to use USB3.0 capture, which I confirm but the app says permission denied and after a moment opening the same permission request window again.

If it's a problem with my capture card, an appropriate error would be preferable. If it is supposed to work, I hope it can be fixed.

Edit:
Response from the dev:
"Please make sure that microphone is not muted in Quick Settings."
Curiously this does solve the issue. But getting an error on USB connectivity when the previous step is really to blame is very misleading. Also I don't think this app should need a microphone permission, please remove it.

As for the performance of the app, I'm only getting between 1~9 fps. When I use the same capture card with my smartphone it for sure doesn't reach the full 60fps either but it isn't as bad as low single digits.

Good start but not good enough to be actually of use. I'm not sure if I should invest in a better card if the current one already doesn't yield full potential performance, points towards the Quest being the limiting factor.

developer_response.body: If you experience infinite loop of USB permission, please make sure that your microphone is not muted in Quick Settings as a workaround. We are working on a fix to address this issue.

reviews.review_description:
The capture card I was previously stuck to 3-8fps on is now working flawlessly with 720p/60fps chosen in the drop-down menu that was added in a recent update. I don’t see an option for 1080p/30fps but that would be nice to have, though the 720p looks nice. 

With this app the Quest 3 becomes a genuinely useful utility, being able to directly hook any HDMI device up opens up a world of possibilities. I’ve enjoyed testing it with my Canon RP and RF200-800mm lens to see a giant live view feed of my superzoom lens, for backyard bird watching/photography it is honestly incredible. 

Please Meta give the person who came up with this one a big raise and don’t stop working on the updates, it has the potential for a lot of enterprise use as this is essentially backwards compatibility with old tech.

reviews.review_title: Not Working no matter what I try
reviews.review_description: Tried two different capture cards but both aren't being read by the Quest.  Had to go through various hoops to get the app since it isn't exactly available on the regular app store. Please do fix this as it's arguably the most requested feature. A easy to understand tutorial would be helpful as well. Appreciate you guys.

I'm using the elgato 4k 60 cam link capture card and although it doesn't charge the quest while i use the card it works flawlessly, what i have discovered is its not the capture card its what source is connected to the device that determines the FPS i used my xbox and ps5 and got 60 FPS used my Ipad and Iphone and it was 9 FPS also if your capture card is not displaying properly as i ran into this issue just restart the source device  and the quest 3 and wow! now i'm playing animal crossing new horizon on a 100 inch screen coming from my nintendo switch into my quest 3 headset. one last thing i have tested Nintendo switch oled, xbox series x, Ps5, Laptop and my Apple TV and its crazy my best feature is watching Apple TV all apps a go on a 100 plus inch screen Great Job Meta.
